insights: Life expectancy peaked in 2019 before the pandemic.
The 2019-2021 decline erased about five years of progress.
report: Global life expectancy rose steadily from 66.8 years in 2000 to a peak of 73.1 in 2019, then fell to 71.4 in 2021 as the COVID-19 pandemic reversed roughly five years of gains.
title: Global Life Expectancy at Birth
subtitle: Years — 2000 to 2021 (WHO global estimate)

methodology.collectionMethod: WHO member-state reporting and modelled estimates.
methodology.coverage: Global aggregate across all WHO regions.

title: Global Healthy Life Expectancy (HALE)
subtitle: Years lived in good health — 2000 to 2021
report: Healthy life expectancy — years lived in good health — followed the same arc as overall life expectancy, peaking at 63.5 years in 2019 before declining to 61.9 in 2021.
insights: HALE tracks closely with overall life expectancy.
The gap between living and living-in-good-health is roughly 9-10 years.

methodology.collectionMethod: WHO member-state reporting and modelled estimates.
methodology.coverage: Global aggregate across all WHO regions.

insights: Europe and the Western Pacific lead; Africa trails.
The regional gap remains over 13 years.
report: In 2021 life expectancy ranged from 63.2 years in the African Region to 76.3 in the European Region — a 13-year gap reflecting persistent global health inequality.
title: Life Expectancy by WHO Region
subtitle: Years at birth — 2021

methodology.collectionMethod: WHO member-state reporting and modelled estimates.
methodology.coverage: Six WHO regions, latest available year (2021).

insights: Healthier Populations exceeded its 1 billion target.
Universal Health Coverage is furthest behind at 431 million.
report: Of WHO's three flagship 'billion' targets, only Healthier Populations has been exceeded (1.4 billion reached against a 1 billion goal). Universal Health Coverage and Health Emergencies Protection both remain off-track.
title: WHO Triple Billion Targets — Progress
subtitle: Millions of people reached — 2024 progress

methodology.collectionMethod: WHO member-state reporting and modelled estimates.
methodology.coverage: WHO GPW13 monitoring, 2024 progress against 2025 targets.
methodology.caveats: Two of three targets remain off-track.

insights: Maternal and child mortality have fallen sharply but remain above target.
Most indicators need faster progress to hit 2030 goals.
report: Most health-related SDG indicators have improved since their baselines but are assessed as needing acceleration to meet 2030 targets. Only alcohol per-capita consumption is currently on track.
title: Health-Related SDG Indicators (Latest)
subtitle: Latest reported value per indicator — toward 2030 targets

methodology.collectionMethod: WHO member-state reporting and modelled estimates.
methodology.coverage: Health-related SDG indicators, latest reported year per indicator.
methodology.caveats: Indicators with non-numeric latest values are omitted from the chart.

insights: Africa's under-5 mortality is roughly 8x Europe's.
Child survival remains the clearest regional divide.
report: Under-5 mortality varies more than eight-fold across WHO regions, from 8 per 1,000 live births in Europe to 67 in Africa — among the starkest health inequalities globally.
title: Under-5 Mortality by WHO Region
subtitle: Deaths per 1,000 live births — latest available

methodology.collectionMethod: WHO member-state reporting and modelled estimates.
methodology.coverage: Six WHO regions, latest available year.

insights: Africa's maternal mortality is ~40x Europe's.
The gap dwarfs that of most other health indicators.
report: Maternal mortality shows the widest regional disparity of any indicator: 442 deaths per 100,000 live births in the African Region versus 11 in Europe — a 40-fold difference.
title: Maternal Mortality by WHO Region
subtitle: Deaths per 100,000 live births — latest available

methodology.collectionMethod: WHO member-state reporting and modelled estimates.
methodology.coverage: Six WHO regions, latest available year.

insights: Education gaps (~19pp) exceed economic and geographic gaps.
The pattern holds across all income groups.
report: Education is the single largest driver of childhood immunization inequality: across income groups, DTP3 coverage gaps tied to a mother's education hover near 19 percentage points — far wider than economic or urban-rural gaps.
title: DTP3 Vaccine Inequality by Education
subtitle: Coverage gap (percentage points) by mother's education — 2014-2023

methodology.collectionMethod: WHO member-state reporting and modelled estimates.
methodology.caveats: Gaps measured in percentage points between highest and lowest strata.

insights: Adoption is now near two-thirds of humanity.
Growth has slowed to incremental yearly additions from a vast base.
report: Social media users grew from roughly 25 million in 2000 to about 5.65 billion in 2026 — a 226-fold increase that now covers roughly 65% of the world's population.
source: DataReportal / company filings, compiled in 'Social Media User Adoption 2000-2026'

methodology.source: DataReportal / company filings, compiled in 'Social Media User Adoption 2000-2026'
methodology.collectionMethod: Compiled from platform filings and industry trackers.
methodology.coverage: Global monthly active users unless noted.
methodology.caveats: Figures are approximate; methodologies differ across platforms.

title: Total Social Media Users Worldwide
subtitle: Monthly active users (billions) — 2000 to 2026

insights: Facebook was the first platform to reach 1 billion users.
Growth has effectively plateaued since 2022.
report: Facebook grew from 100 million users in 2008 to over 3 billion by 2023, becoming the first social network to pass one billion monthly users in 2012. Growth has since flattened near 3.07 billion.
source: DataReportal / company filings, compiled in 'Social Media User Adoption 2000-2026'

methodology.source: DataReportal / company filings, compiled in 'Social Media User Adoption 2000-2026'
methodology.collectionMethod: Compiled from platform filings and industry trackers.
methodology.coverage: Global monthly active users unless noted.
methodology.caveats: Figures are approximate; methodologies differ across platforms.

title: Facebook Monthly Active Users
subtitle: Millions of MAU — 2008 to 2025

insights: Fastest platform ever to reach 1 billion users.
Achieved this despite being banned in India, its largest early market.
report: TikTok reached one billion monthly users within five years of its global launch — the fastest in history, versus eight years for Facebook and nearly ten for Instagram.
source: DataReportal / company filings, compiled in 'Social Media User Adoption 2000-2026'

methodology.source: DataReportal / company filings, compiled in 'Social Media User Adoption 2000-2026'
methodology.collectionMethod: Compiled from platform filings and industry trackers.
methodology.coverage: Global monthly active users unless noted.
methodology.caveats: Figures are approximate; methodologies differ across platforms.

title: TikTok Monthly Active Users
subtitle: Millions of MAU — 2017 to 2026

insights: Seven platforms now exceed one billion users.
Meta's apps together reach over 6 billion monthly.
report: Seven platforms each exceed one billion monthly users in 2026, led by Facebook, WhatsApp, and Instagram — three of which belong to Meta.
source: DataReportal / company filings, compiled in 'Social Media User Adoption 2000-2026'

methodology.source: DataReportal / company filings, compiled in 'Social Media User Adoption 2000-2026'

methodology.caveats: Figures are approximate; methodologies differ across platforms.

title: Largest Social Platforms by Users (2026)
subtitle: Monthly active users (millions) — billion-user club

insights: Short-form video commands the most attention.
Professional and discovery platforms see far shorter sessions.
report: TikTok leads daily engagement at 58 minutes per user, ahead of YouTube at 49. The average internet user spends about 141 minutes daily across all social media.
source: DataReportal / company filings, compiled in 'Social Media User Adoption 2000-2026'

methodology.source: DataReportal / company filings, compiled in 'Social Media User Adoption 2000-2026'

methodology.coverage: Global monthly active users unless noted.
methodology.caveats: Figures are approximate; methodologies differ across platforms.

title: Daily Time Spent by Platform (2026)
subtitle: Average minutes per day per user

insights: Eastern Asia is near-saturated; Africa is largely untapped.
Lowest-penetration regions hold the biggest growth potential.
report: Penetration ranges from 88% in Eastern Asia to under 13% in Eastern Africa, with Southern Asia and Africa representing the largest remaining growth opportunities.
source: DataReportal / company filings, compiled in 'Social Media User Adoption 2000-2026'

methodology.caveats: Figures are approximate; methodologies differ across platforms.

title: Social Media Penetration by Region (2025)
subtitle: Share of population using social media (%)

insights: Social ad spend has grown roughly 20x in a decade.
It now rivals search and display in many markets.
report: Social advertising grew from a $17 billion niche in 2014 to a projected $338 billion in 2026 — nearly a 20-fold expansion, now about a third of all digital ad spending.
source: DataReportal / company filings, compiled in 'Social Media User Adoption 2000-2026'
